prerequisites: A Senseforce platform account with access to the data you want to extract.
Your Senseforce backend platform URL (backend_url), visible on the login screen, for example exampleapi.senseforce.io.
A Senseforce API access token (access_token) generated from your user profile.
The id of an existing Senseforce dataset (dataset_id), visible in the address bar when viewing that dataset in the platform.
A start_date marking the earliest point in time from which to read records.

authMethods.name: Bearer access token
authMethods.summary: Senseforce uses bearer-token authentication. Generate an API access token from your account (click your account in the bottom-left, choose Edit Profile, scroll to the API Access section, and click Generate Access Token), then supply it as the access_token field. Each request sends it as the 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' header, and the token grants the same permissions as the user who created it, so store it as securely as a password.

setupSteps.title: Find your backend URL
setupSteps.body: Identify your Senseforce backend platform URL from the login screen (for example exampleapi.senseforce.io). The public API is served under https://<your-backend-url>/api/. Provide this as the backend_url config field.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API access token
setupSteps.body: In the Senseforce platform, click your account in the bottom-left corner, select Edit Profile, scroll to the API Access section, and click Generate Access Token. Copy the token and provide it as the access_token field. Keep it secure, since anyone holding it can access the platform on your behalf.

setupSteps.title: Identify the dataset to extract
setupSteps.body: Open the dataset you want to sync in the Senseforce Dataset Builder. The dataset's unique id appears in the browser address bar. The API queries it via POST /api/dataset/execute/<id>. Provide this id as the dataset_id field.

setupSteps.title: Set the read window
setupSteps.body: Provide a start_date as the earliest point in time to read records from. Optionally set slice_range to control how the dataset query window is divided into chunks when reading the data.

sources.title: Senseforce 2.0 Manual - Get your access token
